title: overlay: always honor mountProgram
body:
if a mountProgram is specified, use it also in rootfull mode.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3281

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
We wouldn't need to export the two new functions in the overlay package if `setupChrootBindMounts()` was changed to call either `overlay.Mount()` or `overlay.MountWithOptions()`.

> `overlay.MountWithOptions()`.

the issue with that is that it always creates a subdirectory `merge`.  I could try with a new option to skip it creating the subdirectory?

I am fine with such change, but I'll need more time for it.  Probably this PR is not so important and it can wait for the next release without hurrying it up now.

> the issue with that is that it always creates a subdirectory `merge`. I could try with a new option to skip it creating the subdirectory?

looking at it, it seems the new option would cause a conflict with Unmount, since it doesn't accept options and we won't know what subdirectory was used
@giuseppe Would we need a `UnmountWithOptions` as well and probably it could replace generic `Unmount`. But that should be different PR I guess.
I've dropped the patch that passes down the graph options to chroot, as it seems to not be needed to address the linked issue
@giuseppe is this something worth getting into podman 4.0?  Buildah 1.24.1?

> @giuseppe is this something worth getting into podman 4.0? Buildah 1.24.1?

I think it can wait for next release.  The issue happens only with containerized buildah not using a volume on `/var/lib/containers/storage`.
